content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Fair". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/986/problem/A
constraints: 1 <= number of problems solved by each student <= 100
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of students. Then, there are n lines, each containing the name of the student and the number of problems they solved.
content.outputFormat: Output the names of the students who solved the maximum number of problems, one name per line.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Read the input, store the names and the number of problems solved by each student, find the maximum number of problems solved, and output the names of the students who solved that many problems.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
names = []
problems = []
for _ in range(n):
    name, problem = input().split()
    names.append(name)
    problems.append(int(problem))
max_problems = max(problems)
for i in range(n):
    if problems[i] == max_problems:
        print(names[i])
